questions.question: What does "composition" mean in photography?
options: The arrangement of visual elements within the frame
The brightness and contrast of an image
The sharpness of the focal point
The choice of camera and lens
questions.reviewHint: Composition is how the elements in the scene are arranged within the frame to guide the viewer's eye.

questions.question: The rule of thirds divides the frame using…
options: A single line through the centre
Two horizontal and two vertical lines (a 3x3 grid)
A diagonal from corner to corner
A circle in the middle of the frame
questions.reviewHint: The grid of nine equal parts is formed by two horizontal and two vertical lines; place key subjects on the lines or intersections.

questions.question: Where does the rule of thirds suggest you place a key subject or focal point?
options: Always dead centre
On the lines or at their intersections (the "power points")
In the darkest part of the frame
Touching the frame edge
questions.reviewHint: Placing the subject on a third line or intersection tends to feel more balanced than centring it.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of leading lines?
options: To guide the viewer's eye through the photo, often toward the subject
To increase the exposure of the scene
To blur the background
To correct white balance
questions.reviewHint: Lines such as paths, walls or patterns create depth and direct attention toward the main subject.

questions.question: "Framing" as a composition technique means…
options: Using elements in the scene (arches, branches, light) to create a frame around the subject
Printing the photo with a border
Cropping to a square aspect ratio
Setting the camera to its widest focal length
questions.reviewHint: Framing uses elements within the scene - architecture, trees, light and shadow - to surround and emphasise the subject.

questions.question: Negative space in a composition refers to…
options: The empty or uncluttered area around the subject
The darkest shadows in the image
The area that is out of focus
The part of the frame nearest the camera
questions.reviewHint: Clean, empty space around the subject simplifies the image and draws attention to it.

questions.question: A scene feels unbalanced because one large subject sits on the left with nothing on the right. A common fix is to…
options: Counter-weight it with a smaller secondary element or negative space
Increase the ISO
Switch to a longer lens
Move the subject closer to the camera
questions.reviewHint: Balance arranges visual weight across the frame - counter a dominant element with a secondary one or with space.

questions.question: "Layering" a composition (foreground, middle ground, background) mainly adds…
options: A sense of depth and three-dimensionality
More accurate colour
A faster shutter speed
Reduced noise in shadows
questions.reviewHint: Including foreground, middle and background layers creates depth and scale in the image.

questions.question: The page describes the rule of thirds, golden ratio and similar "rules" as…
options: Guidelines that can be adapted or broken for effect, not rigid laws
Strict rules that must never be broken
Settings found in the camera menu
Rules that only apply to landscapes
questions.reviewHint: These are guidelines - they can be adapted or deliberately broken to achieve a desired effect.

questions.question: To make your subject stand out clearly from its background, you should ensure there is…
options: Enough tonal or colour contrast between the subject and the background
A faster frame rate
The subject placed dead centre
The lens stopped down to f/22
questions.reviewHint: A subject separates from its background when there's clear tonal or colour difference between them.

questions.question: When checking the edges of your frame before shooting, you're mainly watching for…
options: Cut-off limbs, awkward tangents or distracting mergers
Correct shutter speed
The histogram shape
Lens fingerprints
questions.reviewHint: Check edges for clipped limbs, tangents and mergers that pull the eye or weaken the composition.
